items.title: SoftReason: A Fully Differentiable Neuro-Soft-Symbolic Deductive Reasoning Architecture over High-Dimensional Perceptual Data
items.abstract: In many reasoning problems, the premises are not observed as discrete symbols, but must be inferred from high-dimensional inputs. Further, the predicate vocabulary, argument structure, and trusted evidence are supplied by a Knowledge Graph (KG), or rule definitions. Classical neuro-symbolic pipeline...

items.title: Persian Pixel: A large-scale synthetic OCR dataset for Persian language
items.abstract: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for Persian remains substantially less mature than for Latin-script languages despite Persian being spoken by more than 110 million people across multiple countries. This gap arises from two fundamental challenges: the intrinsic complexity of the Perso-Arabic writ...

items.title: LKValues: Aligning Large Language Models with Sri Lankan Societal Values
items.authors: Nethmi Muthugala, Supryadi, Surangika Ranathunga et al.
items.abstract: Value alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) has been shown to be culturally biased toward Western norms. This results in the mishandling of local values in multilingual societies such as Sri Lanka that have their unique cultural dynamics. Existing benchmarks overlook Sri Lankan-contextualized va...

items.title: Train the Model, Not the Reader: Decodability Supervision for Verifiable Activation Explanations
items.abstract: Natural-language autoencoders score explanations of hidden activations by reconstruction: an explanation is deemed faithful if the activation can be regenerated from it. The test is structurally insensitive to individual false claims: if flipping a claim does not change the reconstruction, the claim...

items.title: ATSplat: Compact Feed-forward 3D Gaussian Splatting with Adaptive Token Expansion
items.authors: Cho In, Jeonghwan Cho, Mijin Yoo et al.
items.abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) achieves high-quality novel-view synthesis by optimizing freely placed primitives in 3D and adaptively densifying them in under-reconstructed regions. However, this scene-adaptive capacity allocation is largely lost in existing feed-forward 3DGS methods, which commonly r...

items.title: PercepCap: Video Captioner with Structured Spatio-Temporal Perception
items.authors: Yifan Xu, Zihao Wang, Zhixiao Wang et al.
items.abstract: Video captioning requires fine-grained spatio-temporal understanding of videos, including spatial perception of where objects are located and temporal perception of when events occur. Existing MLLMs usually generate captions directly from video inputs without exposing the perceptual evidence behind ...

items.title: Lipschitzian SLLNs for random functions
items.abstract: We prove strong laws of large numbers for locally Lipschitz functions in the Lipschitz pseudometric. Our results hold under either a topological or a model-theoretic condition, with the latter encompassing functions jointly definable in o-minimal structures but extending substantially beyond this cl...

items.title: Towards Miniature Humanoid Tele-Loco-Manipulation Using Virtual Reality and Reinforcement Learning
items.authors: Nicolas Kosanovic, Jordan Dowdy, Jean Chagas Vaz
items.abstract: Full-sized humanoid robot capabilities have grown exponentially in recent years, aiming towards general-purpose deployment in human environments. A popular control method used by manufacturers utilizes Virtual Reality for upper-body teleoperation and Reinforcement Learning for lower-body balance and...

items.title: Distributed Acoustic Localization Array Deployed Using a Soft Everting Vine Robot
items.authors: Sebastian Lorca Godoy, Ciera McFarland, Michael Val et al.
items.abstract: Soft robot exteroception is increasingly being explored for a variety of field applications. In this work, we present a sound-based system for localizing disaster victims in confined and unstructured environments, based on a distributed acoustic sensing architecture embedded along the body of a soft...
